title: Payments and Webhooks
description: Regression tests for checkout/deposit initiation and webhook ingestion surfaces.

tests.title: Patient deposit page can initiate checkout flow without client crashes.
tests.description: This browser test validates that the deposit UI wiring can trigger checkout requests and handle pending states. It is designed to detect frontend integration regressions quickly.
preconditions: payment environment variables are configured for local run
steps: Trigger deposit action with deterministic amount
Assert the UI transitions to expected pending or redirect state
tests.successCriteria: Deposit action does not crash the UI and emits one checkout/deposit API request.

tests.title: Stripe checkout and deposit endpoints handle deterministic requests with stable response contracts.
tests.description: This API test validates request/response behavior of checkout and deposit route handlers under deterministic payloads. It catches status and schema regressions that break frontend payment orchestration.
steps: Assert non-500 status and stable JSON contracts
tests.successCriteria: Both routes respond with non-500 statuses and expected keys for downstream processing.

tests.title: Stripe webhook route rejects malformed payloads and accepts signed deterministic fixtures.
tests.description: This API test validates webhook guard behavior by sending malformed and deterministic signed payloads. It catches insecure acceptance logic and parser regressions in webhook processing.
steps: POST malformed payload to /api/stripe/webhook and verify controlled rejection
POST deterministic valid-like payload and verify non-500 handling path
tests.successCriteria: Malformed payload is rejected without 500 and valid-like deterministic payload is processed by the expected branch.
